title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Below are selected projects that highlight my experience applying marketing analytics to real-world challenges. These projects reflect my ability to combine statistical tools, campaign data, and strategic thinking to inform decisions and drive results.
Whether developing dashboards, analyzing campaign performance, or uncovering insights through SEO and web analytics, each project is rooted in turning complex marketing data into clear, actionable strategies.

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
What You’ll Find

Campaign Performance Analysis: Evaluating the effectiveness of digital marketing efforts using data.
Data Visualization: Turning raw datasets into intuitive dashboards and reports.
Marketing Mix & Attribution: Exploring how different channels contribute to success.
Customer & Market Insights: Using analytics to better understand user behavior and optimize experiences.

title: Thank you

title: Technical Projects
text: Below are selected projects that showcase my work in strategic marketing, campaign development, and brand management. These examples highlight how I apply leadership, data-informed decision making, and creative direction to drive marketing success.

title: Technical Projects
section: TRI Google Ads Optimization & Campaign Strategy Grad Capstone Project (Fall 2025 - Present)
text:
TRI Google Ads Optimization & Campaign Strategy Grad Capstone Project (Fall 2025 - Present)
For my client project with the Trauma Resource Institute (TRI), I am leading the development and optimization of Google Ads campaigns to improve lead generation and maximize the impact of the Google Ads grant.
This work involves auditing historical campaign performance, restructuring campaigns based on user intent, and aligning keywords, ad copy, and landing pages to drive higher-quality conversions.
Skills Demonstrated:

Search Campaign Strategy: Designed intent-based campaign structure (brand vs. non-brand, segmented by program offerings like CRM and TRM).
Keyword & Match Type Optimization: Evaluated performance at the keyword–match level to improve relevance, CTR, and conversion rate.
Conversion-Focused Thinking: Focused on optimizing for meaningful actions (training sign-ups, donations, and inquiries), not just traffic.
Data Analysis & Reporting: Analyzed search term data, user behavior, and campaign metrics to guide weekly optimization decisions.
Cross-Channel Alignment: Ensured consistency between ad messaging and landing page experience to improve conversion performance.

title: Technical Projects
section: IBM6510 Data Analysis Project: Exploratory Data & Insights (Fall 2024)
text:
IBM6510 Data Analysis Project: Exploratory Data & Insights (Fall 2024)
As part of my first semester in graduate school, I led the sourcing and development of a dataset-driven analysis project using R and Quarto. I independently identified and curated a real-world animal shelter dataset, then collaborated with my team to clean, analyze, and interpret the data to uncover meaningful insights.
This project focused on understanding the factors that influence animal adoption outcomes. Through data preparation, feature engineering, and statistical analysis, I examined how variables such as breed type, intake condition, age, and intake source impact the likelihood of adoption.
The analysis included exploratory data analysis, correlation analysis, and logistic regression modeling. During modeling, I identified multicollinearity issues across predictors, which informed how results were interpreted and highlighted limitations in statistical significance despite meaningful variable relationships.
Skills Demonstrated:

Data Sourcing & Preparation: Identified and evaluated an external dataset, then cleaned and structured it using R (tidyverse, janitor, rio).
Feature Engineering: Created binary indicators for breed categories, intake conditions, and adoption outcomes to support modeling.
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA): Analyzed trends in adoption rates across intake type, age, and breed classifications.
Statistical Modeling: Built logistic regression models to evaluate factors influencing adoption likelihood.
Analytical Thinking: Diagnosed multicollinearity and interpreted model limitations in a real-world dataset.
Data Visualization: Used correlation matrices and plots to communicate relationships between key variables.
Reproducible Reporting: Developed a Quarto-based report integrating code, outputs, and narrative insights.



Interactive Report

title: Professional Resume
text:
Experience

EPIC Insurance Brokers & Consultants
Client Coordinator, Employee Benefits
January 2024 – August 2024

Served as the primary point of contact for Client Executives, directly reporting on all requested deliverables to streamline compliance processes and optimize the delivery of marketing materials to clients, ensuring consistent alignment with regulatory standards and client expectations.
Strategically managed a diverse portfolio of approximately 15 large group accounts, each with over 500 employees, alongside multiple smaller divisions, providing personalized support and proactive account management to maintain client satisfaction and retention.
Provided expert assistance in resolving complex client and employee issues, including benefit claim disputes, eligibility concerns, and policy inquiries, ensuring timely resolution and maintaining strong client relationships.
Cultivated and sustained successful, mutually beneficial relationships with a wide network of stakeholders, including clients, insurance carriers, and third-party administrators, fostering collaboration and clear communication.
Leveraged industry-leading BenAdmin platforms, including BenefitPoint, to accurately manage and maintain all policy details, commission structures, and documentation, ensuring transparency, compliance, and data integrity.
Designed and delivered comprehensive financial reports, renewal presentations, and client brochures, incorporating key marketing metrics, benchmarking data, and commission analysis to support informed decision-making and client retention strategies.



Suvaun, Acrisure LLC Agency Partner
Marketing Account Coordinator
March 2020 – January 2024

Spearheaded and led comprehensive pre-sale marketing initiatives for small accounts, driving targeted outreach strategies that effectively captured and engaged prospective clients while maximizing lead conversion.
Strategically managed the end-to-end Request for Proposal (RFP) marketing process, guiding broker users from initial engagement through successful implementation, ensuring a seamless and efficient experience that met client expectations.
Cultivated and maintained strong, strategic relationships with a diverse range of carrier vendors, serving as a trusted liaison between carriers and clients, facilitating clear communication, negotiation, and conflict resolution.
Collaborated cross-functionally with an international outsourcing team, providing expert guidance on leveraging advanced features within Suvaun, overseeing task execution to ensure accuracy, efficiency, and adherence to best practices.
Delivered comprehensive training and onboarding programs for both internal team members and external broker partners, enhancing user proficiency and fostering a strong foundation for long-term success.



L.A. Pinnacle Insurance Agency
Benefits Marketing & Admin Assistant
April 2019 – March 2020

Supported administration of group and individual health insurance plans, ensuring accurate enrollments, terminations, and policy updates.
Managed day-to-day client and employee requests, including claims support, eligibility verification, and billing inquiries.
Coordinated with carriers and vendors to resolve coverage issues and maintain compliance with policy requirements.
Prepared renewal comparisons, rate summaries, and marketing packages to support broker recommendations.
Utilized Excel and Word & Brown to organize client data, track plan details, and streamline reporting processes.

title: Welcome to my Portfolio
text: I’m a Digital Marketing Analytics graduate student pursuing opportunities at the intersection of data, technology, and insurance. With a foundation in client relations, I specialize in transforming complex data into clear, actionable insights that support strategic decision-making and improve customer outcomes.

title: Welcome to my Portfolio
text:
About Me
I’m currently pursuing a Master of Science in Digital Marketing (Marketing Analytics) at Cal Poly Pomona, and hold a Bachelor of Arts in Communications from UC San Diego. I have a background in client relations within the insurance industry, where I supported large group accounts and developed experience in data analysis, reporting, and strategic decision-making.

title: Welcome to my Portfolio
text:
What I Do
I focus on answering questions like:

How can data improve client strategy and decision-making in employee benefits?
How can we better analyze plan performance, costs, and trends across large group accounts?
How can reporting be streamlined to deliver clearer insights for clients and stakeholders?
How can technology and analytics tools be used to modernize traditionally manual processes?
How can we turn complex benefits data into clear, actionable insights?
